summary.intent: Implement Anthropic prompt caching for Moltis, then extend it to match all caching features offered by OpenClaw (the reference implementation), including a configurable cache_retention setting.
summary.outcome: Full prompt caching implemented for Anthropic direct and OpenRouter Anthropic models, with a CacheRetention enum (none/short/long) wired through config → provider registration → request serialization. 404 tests pass, clean build and clippy across the workspace. PR created and pushed.

repo: Anthropic provider is in crates/providers/src/anthropic.rs; OpenAI-compat provider (used for OpenRouter, DeepSeek, etc.) is in crates/providers/src/openai.rs; provider registration with config injection is in crates/providers/src/lib.rs.
Config schema lives in crates/config/src/schema.rs with serde skip-if-default helpers; validate.rs has build_schema_map for TOML key validation; template.rs holds the default config file template.

Provider structs use builder-pattern methods (e.g., with_reasoning_effort, with_cache_retention) rather than direct struct construction, and with_reasoning_effort must propagate new fields to the cloned struct.
OpenRouter Anthropic models are served via OpenAiProvider (not AnthropicProvider); detection is via base_url containing 'openrouter.ai' and model starting with 'anthropic/'.
The prompt-caching-2024-07-31 beta header is no longer required — prompt caching is GA on Anthropic. OpenClaw's pi-ai library confirmed this by not including it.

DeepSeek and OpenAI cache automatically server-side; Ollama/vLLM use KV prefix cache; local GGUF uses llama-cpp-2 Rust bindings with internal KV reuse — none require client-side cache_control markers.
CacheRetention 'long' vs 'short' in OpenClaw maps to TTL hints at the SDK level, not a different cache_control type — both use { type: 'ephemeral' } in the JSON.
Config enum variants use serde rename_all = 'lowercase' pattern; a matching is_default_* helper gates serde skip_serializing_if.

code.finding: to_anthropic_messages() originally returned (Option<String>, Vec<Value>); changed to (Option<Vec<Value>>, Vec<Value>) so system prompt can be sent as a content-block array with cache_control.
complete() sets body['system'] — must handle both Vec<Value> (caching on) and plain String (caching off) branches.
stream_with_tools() mirrors complete() for the system prompt and beta header treatment.

apply_openrouter_cache_control() injects cache_control on system message and last user message; must be called on the prepared messages Vec before it is inserted into the request body, in both complete() and stream_with_tools_sse().
New public types (CacheRetention) must be explicitly re-exported here; they are not auto-exported from schema.rs.
build_schema_map() must be updated with every new ProviderEntry field, otherwise TOML validation rejects the key as unknown.

workflow: OpenClaw source at ~/code/openclaw is the canonical reference; pi-ai compiled output at node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-ai/dist/ reveals exact wire-level behavior that TypeScript source may not make obvious.
Running cargo clippy --package providers after editing only that crate is faster than workspace clippy and avoids unrelated CUDA/cmake failures on macOS.
cargo fmt --check before committing catches blank-line artifacts left by Edit operations; rustfmt --edition 2021 fixes them.
Tracing spans should include config-derived flags (e.g., caching = caching) so behavior is observable without adding separate log statements.

friction: Multiple rounds of partial reads of large files (openai.rs, anthropic.rs) were needed to find exact insertion points — the file is large enough that a single read misses context.
Removing the anthropic-beta header left stray blank lines that required a follow-up edit and re-format cycle.
Workspace-level clippy fails due to an unrelated CUDA/cmake build issue on macOS, forcing per-crate clippy invocations.
with_reasoning_effort clones the provider struct, so every new field must be explicitly propagated there — easy to miss and not caught until compile time.
open_items: CacheRetention 'long' variant is accepted in config but behaves identically to 'short' at runtime — TTL-hint behavior (longer cache lifetime) is not yet implemented.
Bedrock Anthropic caching is not implemented because Moltis has no Bedrock provider; when Bedrock is added, cache_control injection should be wired the same way as direct Anthropic.
Cache diagnostics are debug-log only; a structured cache trace (JSONL per-turn cache hit/miss log like OpenClaw's diagnostics.cacheTrace) could be added later.
Context pruning ('cache-ttl' mode — pruning stale tool results after cache TTL expires) is an advanced OpenClaw optimization that was explicitly deferred.
Heartbeat keep-warm (periodic pings to prevent cache eviction on long-idle sessions) was noted as a future enhancement.
No UI surface for cache_retention config — users must edit TOML directly; a settings panel toggle could be added later.
